comments: Tommy has a repeated session from Demon.

track_listing.track: Allied Forces (LP - Allied Forces) RCA Victor
I Can't Go For That (No Can Do) (LP - Private Eyes) RCA
Over The Top (LP - Rock Until You Drop) Neat
Fire In The Sky (LP - Denim And Leather) Carrere
Life In Tokyo (LP - Assemblage) Hansa International
Laugh Till You Cry... Live Till You Die (LP - InCANdescence) Virgin
Despiser (LP - End Of The World) Rondelet Music & Records
Heavy Metal (v/a LP - Heavy Metal - Music From The Motion Picture) Epic
Awakening (LP - Freedom At Point Zero) Grunt

transcript.text: National Radio 1. Well, hello there. This is TV on the Radio and here's Thomas the Vance and welcome once again to The Friday Rock Show.
Right, tonight we catch up on loads of new releases because there are quite a few about and I've crammed in as many as possible tonight.
Also, during the course of the next couple of hours, we repeat our recent session by the band called Demon.
Talking about the new stuff, I've got stuff by Triumph, Atomic Rooster, Greg Lake, Hall & Oates, Ozzy Osbourne and Y&T.
Also Raven, Saxon, Starfighters, Gaskins, Sammy Hagar, Tank, Mother's Ruin, and Girl.
A Friday night connection, a bit of a strange one, but you'll hear about that later.
We start with this.
That's the A-side of the new single by Atomic Rooster that's just come out on the Polydor label.
You can get it as a three-tracker, 12-incher as well, and every single track is excellent.
That one was called Play It Again.
And before that, something from the new album by Triumph, that three-piece band out of Canada.
You heard the title track of their new album, which is out on RCA, Allied Forces.
Twelve minutes after ten, the Friday Rock Show.
A little message here from somebody who lives in Salisbury, a lady by the name of Sarah.
She says, would Andy, better known as Oily to his friends, get in touch with Sarah from Salisbury?
It seems they met briefly at the Reading Festival this year, and she can't forget you, Oily.
Happy 30th birthday to a gentleman by the name of David Bailey.
Not the photographer, he thinks, from his wife, June Bailey.
I've got to tell you, sir, that the whole family loves you a lot.
That includes your baby daughter, Stephanie, as well.
Karen Mellor, she lives in Dundee.
Tells me that her husband, Paul, listens to the programme up till about 11 o'clock,
and after that he goes to sleep as he listens in bed.
Then from me and from Stephen, who's six months,
Park.
Nice little letter from Debbie McGulkin, I think it is,
a 16, Twinburn Park, Monkston, Newton Abbey County,
entered Northern Ireland.
Wanted to hear a track by Triumph and just did.
Please say he says, she says, from the Zaha to Jillian, Jerry and Smith.
And also Catherine, who got her hair cut.
This is Greg Lake.
Finest concerts I think I've ever seen was put together by those two guys at the Hammersmith Odeon a few years ago.
Daryl Hall and John Oates, a track from their brand new album, which has the title Private Eyes.
The track you heard there was called I Can't Go For That.
Before that, you heard something from Greg Lake from the new Greg Lake Band.
The album's out on Chrysalis.
The track you heard was Long Goodbye.
Greg Lake is a guest tomorrow on Rock On 5 to 6.30.
That'll hear from Wheelie Bong of Easton, Wirral, Merseyside.
He wants me to say hello to Duncan Graham Sourness, I think it is, lookalike.
And Sid from Rotherham, whom we think actually works in a bean factory.
Hi to Andy Gratton and Richie Major from Exeter.
Tell him we appreciate the trip to Dartmoor.
Hope he wasn't a nick.
Cheers to the barman at the Holiday Inn somewhere down in Dawlish, I think.
And thanks to George for the help at the Rainbow Gig.
and finally, grapes to the fellow who worked at the welcome shop.
A lonely lady by the name of 515 at Wolverhampton, West Midlands.
Good evening.
Nigel Sharp has got a birthday today.
He's 21.
Actually, that's a lie.
His birthday was on the 30th of September.
He wants me to say hello to Julia, his wife, whom he loves very much.
They live in Erith in Kent.

can I give a quick mention to Pete Filbert and Joss Oswald Harvey Headbanger,

whom everybody sort of met at the marquee gig

a live version of Suicide Solution.
